root_cause.text: The bounds check in `decode_frame` was insufficient because it did not account for the additional 1024 bytes that could be read during slice processing, leading to an out-of-array read.
root_cause.evidence: The patch updates the check `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < slice_end` to `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < slice_end + 1024LL`, ensuring that an extra 1024 bytes are available in the buffer.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The bounds check in `decode_frame` was insufficient because it did not account for the additional 1024 bytes that could be read during slice processing, leading to an out-of-array read.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch updates the check `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < slice_end` to `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < slice_end + 1024LL`, ensuring that an extra 1024 bytes are available in the buffer.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a padding margin to the slice size validation to prevent out-of-bounds reads.
intent_record.what: Added a 1024-byte margin to the slice boundary check.
intent_record.how: Ensures that there are at least 1024 extra bytes available beyond the slice end to prevent potential out-of-bounds reads during decoding.

grouping_result.intent: Add a padding margin to the slice size validation to prevent out-of-bounds reads.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by adding a padding margin to the slice size validation, which accounts for the additional bytes and prevents the out-of-bounds read.

candidates.rationale: This line is part of the bounds check that was insufficient and did not account for the padding margin.
This line contains the specific bounds check logic that failed to account for the additional 1024 bytes, allowing an out-of-bounds read.

vuln_statements.explanation: This line is the insufficient bounds check that fails to account for the additional padding (1024 bytes), leading to the out-of-bounds read.
This is the first part of the insufficient bounds check condition.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
avcodec/utvideodec: fix multiple slices for UQY2 and other issues

Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 3ecc59bc35412d0cc2299668dd1f79fff090eb3f
Author: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 11 21:31:49 2016 +0200

    avcodec/utvideodec: fix multiple slices for UQY2 and other issues
    
    Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>

diff --git a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
index 5c6c318a01..4544b06272 100644
--- a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
+++ b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
@@ -494,28 +494,36 @@ static int decode_frame(AVCodecContext *avctx, void *data, int *
bic_trace_step.reason: The function performs a bounds check `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < slice_end` but then skips both `plane_size` (which is `slice_end`) and an additional 1024 bytes via `bytestream2_skipu`. It fails to ensure that there are at least `slice_end + 1024` bytes available, leaving it vulnerable to out-of-array reads.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
avcodec/utvideo: add support for UQY2

Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 115e63c8d6791ebdc921caf40c3def024690af2e
Author: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 11 18:17:41 2016 +0200

    avcodec/utvideo: add support for UQY2
    
    Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>

diff --git a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
index c31416b21b..5c6c318a01 100644
--- a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
+++ b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
@@ -35,6 +35,50 @@
 #include "thread.h"
 #include "utvideo.h"
 
+static int build_huff10(const uint8_t *src, VLC *
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `decode_frame` logic for `c->pro` format with `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < 1024 + 4 * c->slices`. However, the root cause mentions an out-of-array read due to an insufficient bounds check not accounting for additional 1024 bytes during slice processing. In the `else` branch (which handles non-`c->pro` frames), the bounds check remains `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < 256 + 4 * c->slices`, which is insufficient and does not account for the additional 1024 bytes (or the pro branch might have the 1024, but the check for non-pro is missing it). Furthermore, `decode_plane10` is introduced, reading from `plane_start[i + 1] - 1024` without adequate guarantee. Thus, the vulnerable construct is present and not fixed in this commit.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Ut Video decoder

Signed-off-by: Anton Khirnov <anton@khirnov.net>
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided snippet is an initialization function (likely decode_init), not decode_frame. The vulnerable bounds check logic described in the root cause is completely absent from this code.

diff --git a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c

diff --git a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c b/libavcodec/utvideodec.c
index c31416b21b..5c6c318a01 100644
--- a/libavcodec/utvideodec.c

@@ -35,6 +35,50 @@

bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `decode_plane10` and `c->pro` mode for the UQY2 format, adding a bounds check `bytestream2_get_bytes_left(&gb) < 1024 + 4 * c->slices`.
